assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies Service A as HIGH RISK and states 'Will block upgrades - No pods can be evicted' due to minAvailable: 100%

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Response identifies Service B as HIGH RISK and explicitly states 'maxUnavailable: 0 and minAvailable: 100% are equivalent — both prevent ANY pod eviction'

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Response rates Service C as MEDIUM RISK and states it 'May block if multi-zone', not as safe. It correctly identifies that only 1 pod can be evicted but presents this as risky rather than safe

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies Service D as LOW RISK/Upgrade-safe and explains it 'Allows 1 pod eviction per drain cycle' with 4 healthy replicas remaining

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific YAML configuration showing 'minAvailable: 2' and notes 'or use maxUnavailable: 1' for Service A

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific YAML configuration with 'maxUnavailable: 1' for Service B

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE respects PDB constraints for up to 1 hour' and 'After 1 hour: GKE force-evicts remaining pods, bypassing PDB protection'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl patch command: 'kubectl patch pdb SERVICE_PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE -p '{"spec":{"maxUnavailable":"50%"}}'

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple references to testing: 'Test all PDB changes in a dev cluster upgrade first' and 'Test PDB settings in dev/staging environments during upgrade rehearsals'
